rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. TRINITY PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Class Certification
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 10 - PLAINTIFF ANNE THAYER, ERNEST THAYER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Class Certification. Plaintiff's Motion for Class Certification. Hearing Required. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE WOOLDRIDGE VS. ARTHUR STEVEN BROWN, ALSO KNOWN AS STEVE BROWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 1 - PLAINTIFF NICOLE WOOLDRIDGE MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Admission/Interrogatories As to Norman Sabel - Granted as requested; As to Sylvia Sabel - granted as follows: defendant shall provide adequate verifications for original and first supplemental responses; As to Arthur Steven Brown - granted as follows: defendant shall provide adequate verifications for original and first supplemental responses; granted as to SPROG 24 and FROG 4.1 (insurance) and 50.5 (attorney-client privilege not applicable and "legal conclusion" objection is not timely). As to Sabel Properties, LLC - parties are ordered to appear at 9 a.m. to engage in further meet and confer efforts. It appears that Defendant attempted to provide supplemental responses. Ex. 6 to Defendants' Opposition; Sanctions granted as follows: $5,000 as to Defendant Norman Sabel and his counsel only; $4,500 jointly and severally as to defendants Sylvia Sabel, Sabel Properties, LLC and Brown and their counsel. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CHICO MALL INVESTORS LLC VS. CHINESE GOURMET GROUP INC.,
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 2 - CROSS DEFENDANT CHICO MALL INVESTORS LLC DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant's Demurrer to Second Amended Cross-Complaint is continued to April 29, 2013 per agreement of the parties. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MAKI KAYANO VS. DAVID WALZER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID WALZER's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT 1st c/a for constructive eviction- OVERRULED, Plaintiff alleges that she was deprived of "beneficial enjoyment" of the premises; 2nd and 3rd c/a for IIED and NIED- SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege facts amounting to outrageous behavior; 4th c/a for breach of warranty of habitability - SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege facts showing that Defendant's actions rendered premises rented to Plaintiff uninhabitable; 5th and 6th c/a for negligence and nuisance - OVERRULE, all elements are sufficiently alleged; 7th c/a - SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority fort he proposition that "Eligible Tenant's" 12 month period includes period after service of Notice. 8th c/a for breach of contract- SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to specify provisions of the contract allegedly breached. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MAKI KAYANO VS. DAVID WALZER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANT DAVID WALZER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege facts supporting request for punitive damages. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE E. SANTOS COLORADO VS. THE BANK OF MELLON NEW YORK et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend to separate causes of action. To the extent Complaint seeks to enforce mechanic's lien, Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege, in good faith, extension of credit pursuant to Civil Code 8460(b); if such amendment cannot be made demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as complaint was not filed within 90 days of recordation of lien. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD R BULAN VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Defendants Jp Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. And California Reconveyance Company To Plaintiffs' Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 11. Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Defendants Jp Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. And California Reconveyance Company To Plaintiffs' Complaint Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs' complaint is barred by res judicata as it arises out of the same facts as Complaint brought in and dismissed with prejudice by District Court on April 27, 2011. No opposition filed. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASOCIATION VS. RANDALL B ALLISON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT RANDALL ALLISON and YOLANDA ALLISON's Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery GRANTED, subject to opposition. Discovery is relevant to Plaintiff's case under CCP 1161a(b)(3). See Complaint 5. Grant sanctions $1,500 in attorney's fees and $60 costs. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: QUINCY YU et al VS. JEFFREY YU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deft To Allow Inspection Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, April 19, 2013, line 7. PLAINTIFF QUINCY YU and KAY YU's Motion To Compel Defendant To Allow Inspection Of Real Property DENIED. Insufficient notice. Notice is made by filing and serving. Motion was filed on April 15, 2013 and Noticed for April 19, 2013. CCP 1005, 1170.8. = (501/AJR)
